question.text: Under FEMA, serious forex offences including money laundering and round-tripping may also attract provisions of which legislation?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Serious forex offences under FEMA, including money laundering and round-tripping of funds, can also be prosecuted under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA), 2002. This creates a dual enforcement framework with both civil (FEMA) and criminal (PMLA) consequences. **The Trap:** The "Indian Penal Code" covers general criminal offences, not specifically financial crimes related to forex. PMLA is the targeted legislation for money laundering connected to FEMA violations. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX and NISM AML/CFT certification both test the FEMA-PMLA intersection. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** In cross-border transactions, always conduct enhanced due diligence on fund flows — round-tripping (money going out and coming back as FDI) triggers both FEMA and PMLA investigations.
